recommendations.deficiency: The 208/120V panelboard D in the gym storage area had a detached dead front cover that was leaning against an adjacent panelboard, leaving electrical wiring exposed. This configuration poses a significant electrical safety hazard to facility personnel and building occupants. The panelboard had exceeded its expected useful life, reducing serviceability and reliability while increasing the risk of failure.
recommendations.recommendation: Immediately secure the dead front cover to the panelboard D with proper hardware to eliminate electrical exposure hazards and ensure code compliance.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – A Critical Impact

recommendations.deficiency: One bugeye-style emergency light fixture in the lobby had a broken lamp, compromising emergency egress illumination in this critical area. Proper emergency lighting is essential for safe building evacuation. The emergency lights had exceeded its expected useful life, reducing serviceability and reliability while increasing the risk of failure.
recommendations.recommendation: Replace the broken lamp in the emergency light fixture immediately to restore required emergency egress illumination and ensure life safety compliance.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – A Critical Impact

recommendations.recommendation: Properly secure all VFD covers to eliminate electrical exposure and ensure safe access for maintenance activities.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The ceiling tiles in weight room WEIGHTRM covering 767 square feet showed mismatched tiles and water staining, indicating past moisture problems. This condition affects both appearance and may indicate ongoing moisture issues. The ceiling finishes had exceeded their expected useful life resulting in reduced durability and appearance, with an increased likelihood of continued wear, deterioration, and the need for replacement.
recommendations.recommendation: Investigate and repair any moisture sources, then replace all stained and mismatched ceiling tiles to provide a uniform appearance and proper ceiling system function.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Building Envelope / Environmental Protection – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The modified bitumen roof covering throughout the entire roof area exhibited moderate surface degranulation. This condition indicates normal weathering but may affect the roof's ability to shed water effectively and protect against UV degradation.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor the roof condition closely and plan for roof replacement within the next 5-7 years, or apply protective coating to extend the roof's service life.

recommendations.deficiency: The roof drains had exceeded their expected useful life resulting in reduced durability and appearance, with an increased likelihood of continued wear, deterioration, and the need for replacement.

recommendations.deficiency: Facility maintenance staff reported that the concrete floor in the freezer/cooler area had experienced differential settlement and sinking, resulting in uneven floor surfaces.
recommendations.recommendation: Recommend evaluation by a qualified structural engineer or geotechnical contractor to determine the extent and cause of the differential movement. Repairs should be completed as necessary, which may include slab stabilization, subgrade correction, drainage/moisture mitigation, and replacement or leveling of affected floor sections to restore a safe and serviceable condition.
recommendations.estimateSow: Slab stabilization and partial replacement allowance
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Approximately 75% of the 40,000 square feet of asphalt paving throughout the site exhibited significant alligator cracking and surface deterioration with potholes observed at the kitchen entrance and bus loop areas. The roadways & parking had exceeded their expected useful life, reducing serviceability and reliability while increasing the risk of failure.
recommendations.recommendation: Implement comprehensive pavement rehabilitation including pothole repairs, crack sealing, and overlay application to restore safe vehicular and pedestrian circulation.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Maintenance staff reported that while no major operational concerns exist with the site electrical distribution system, the infrastructure is significantly aged and exhibits capacity deficiencies for current campus needs.
recommendations.recommendation: Develop electrical system upgrade plan to address capacity limitations and replace aging components before operational failures occur.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Operational Continuity / Building Systems – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Multiple storm drainage components throughout the site were obstructed by debris with missing covers on cleanouts, and erosion in the courtyard area contributes to system blockages and reported flooding conditions.
recommendations.recommendation: Clear all drainage obstructions, install missing cleanout covers, implement erosion control measures, and establish regular maintenance schedule to prevent future blockages.
recommendations.estimateSow: Clean inlets, replace covers, erosion repairs
recommendations.impactSeverity: Building Envelope / Environmental Protection – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: All three detention pond inlet and outlet structures were blocked with obstructions and showed physical damage, severely compromising stormwater management capacity and system effectiveness.

recommendations.recommendation: Trim back tree branches to eliminate contact with the roof surface and inspect affected areas for damage that may require patching or repair.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Building Envelope / Environmental Protection – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The concrete stairs with painted handrails near AHU15 showed moderate material deterioration with chipped and peeling paint on approximately 15 linear feet of handrails and guardrails. This condition compromises both the protective coating and aesthetic appearance of the safety features.
recommendations.recommendation: Remove loose paint, prepare surfaces, and apply new protective coating to the handrails and guardrails to restore proper finish and prevent further deterioration of the underlying materials.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Thirty linear feet of concrete handrails with painted finish along the exterior ramp showed deteriorated surface paint throughout. This condition exposes the underlying material to weather and reduces the aesthetic appearance of the facility.
recommendations.recommendation: Prepare the handrail surfaces and apply new paint coating to protect against weather exposure and restore appearance.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The attached canopy structural framing members showed deteriorated surface paint allowing moderate rust corrosion throughout 340 square feet. Rust corrosion is also present on the underside of the metal roof deck, compromising the structural integrity of the canopy system.
recommendations.recommendation: Remove rust corrosion, prime, and repaint all affected structural framing members and roof deck surfaces to prevent further deterioration and maintain structural integrity.
recommendations.estimateSow: Prep, prime, repaint rusted canopy framing/deck
recommendations.impactSeverity: Building Envelope / Environmental Protection – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The grass football field showed drainage problems with ponding water observed at multiple locations across the playing surface following precipitation events.
recommendations.recommendation: Improve field drainage through installation of subsurface drainage systems and grading corrections to eliminate standing water conditions.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Educational Delivery / Learning Environment – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Approximately 1,000 linear feet representing 33% of the perimeter chain link fencing system exhibited damaged, corroded, loose, and missing components compromising site security and safety.
recommendations.recommendation: Replace damaged fence sections, repair loose components, and apply corrosion protection treatment to restore perimeter security effectiveness.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Security – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Concrete pavement near the track exhibited cracking and breakage across approximately 1,000 square feet, creating trip hazards and compromising pedestrian circulation safety.
recommendations.recommendation: Remove and replace damaged concrete sections with proper joint installation to restore safe pedestrian access and prevent further pavement failure.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Approximately 1,000 square feet of concrete sidewalk adjacent to Building D exhibited surface and base material deterioration due to stormwater runoff erosion, compromising pedestrian safety.
recommendations.recommendation: Repair deteriorated concrete sections, improve drainage control, and implement erosion protection measures to prevent further sidewalk deterioration.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Three gates at various locations exhibited corroded, damaged, loose, and missing components including main structural bars, support posts, and fence fabric, compromising access control and security.
recommendations.recommendation: Repair or replace damaged gate components, install new hardware, and apply corrosion protection to restore proper gate operation and security function.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Security – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Concrete sidewalk between Buildings B and D showed erosion of base material across approximately 100 linear feet, undermining structural integrity and creating safety concerns.
recommendations.recommendation: Excavate and replace deteriorated base material, install proper drainage control, and reconstruct affected sidewalk sections to restore structural integrity.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The concrete swale west of Building D showed signs of drainage path failure and ponding across approximately 250 square feet, preventing proper stormwater conveyance.
recommendations.recommendation: Regrade concrete swale to restore proper drainage flow and eliminate ponding conditions that compromise stormwater management effectiveness.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Building Envelope / Environmental Protection – C Localized Impact

recommendations.deficiency: The tennis court backstop system had missing panels across approximately 10 square feet of protective screening, creating safety hazards and reducing court functionality.
recommendations.recommendation: Install replacement backstop panels to restore court safety and prevent ball migration beyond court boundaries.
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – C Localized Impact

recommendations.deficiency: Approximately 50 square feet of concrete sidewalk in the courtyard between Buildings D and B exhibited differential settlement cracking, indicating foundation instability and creating trip hazards.
recommendations.recommendation: Investigate underlying settlement causes, stabilize subgrade conditions, and replace affected concrete sections to eliminate safety hazards and prevent further settlement.
recommendations.estimateSow: Replace settled sidewalk and stabilize subgrade
recommendations.impactSeverity: Health & Life Safety – B Significant Impact
